title: Optimize Total Cost per Accepted Change
status_reason: The accepted-change economics implementation, deterministic route planning, sequential controller integration, online evidence update, policy replay/publication/rollback, contracts, CLI, Agents presentation, and deterministic validation are complete. PT8 cannot be reported COMPLETE because the frozen founder input contains zero eligible historical cases and Gate C remains INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE; conservative reliability and false-acceptance exposure therefore cannot be proven for a production policy publication.

gate_c.reason: The isolated PT8 Gate C run had no configured scorecards, while the durable PT7 Gate C scorecards show zero eligible Villani Code, Codex, and Claude Code observations. Neither artifact can support qualification or policy activation.

policy_evaluation.reason: There are no point-in-time founder cases, so conservative reliability, false-acceptance exposure, cost, time, review, escalation, and regret cannot be compared without fabrication.

migrated: Newly initialized configuration receives the additive villani.accepted_change_economics_configuration.v1 block and accepted_change_economics_v1 policy. Existing configuration without that block remains readable and does not gain online-update side effects.
Run manifests gained optional route_plans and economics_update artifact pointers; older bundles remain valid and readable.
Qualification source_kind gained canonical_run so only validated finalized run projections can enter future evidence.
Seven additive normative/packaged v1 contracts and eight generated protocol fixtures were added; no existing contract version was replaced.
The Web production bundle and synchronized Agentd console assets were refreshed from one verified build.
files.scope_note: The worktree also contains the immediately preceding user-authorized PT6 and PT7 changes documented in their completion reports. This list identifies PT8 additions and PT8-touched shared files; those earlier changes were preserved.

architecture_and_product_decisions: Classification remains before route planning. The controller builds a content-addressed route plan only from task assessment, repository identity, pre-run qualification/economics evidence, availability, budgets, privacy, provider, permission, and environment constraints.

The objective is (execution + verification + expected human review + retry/escalation + latency penalty) divided by conservative acceptance probability. Complete totals are compared only when all required inputs are authoritative and currency-compatible; otherwise the plan records a partial objective and its unknowns.

Acceptance probability uses conservative repository/task qualification evidence. Qualified systems are the automatic candidate set; the strongest eligible provisional route is an explicit sparse fallback; experimental systems are never automatic.

When comparable complete objectives exist, the optimizer chooses the lowest conservative accepted-change economics. Sparse evidence falls back to strongest qualified evidence rather than treating missing inputs as zero.

Local-only, Local first, provider allow/preference, exclusion, permission, maximum known cost, forced system, and strongest-only controls are explicit route constraints. The route picker stays hidden by default, and forced choices are labeled and excluded from automatic-policy quality metrics.

Sequential behavior reuses the deterministic controller: same-system retry requires credible progress plus an actionable correction; capability failure or lack of credible progress escalates to a qualified stronger fallback; verification and final-validation reserves remain protected. PT8 adds no arbitrary retry loop.

Every decision persists a versioned route-plan artifact with systems considered, state, probability, component costs/duration, unknowns, rejections, first route, fallbacks, sequence economics, reserves, policy/input digests, and explanation.

After finalization, an explicitly enabled projector appends only eligible authoritative canonical-run outcomes to qualification and economics stores. Updates are future-only; infrastructure-excluded or unverified outcomes are recorded as exclusions/skips, forced metrics are excluded, and false acceptance appends an immediate qualification invalidation.

Policy evaluation replays frozen cases point-in-time, emits four strategy scorecards, and refuses publication unless conservative reliability is non-decreasing and false-acceptance exposure is non-increasing. Publications are immutable and the active pointer supports deterministic rollback. No LLM can publish controller policy.
Semantic verification remains blind to harness identity, route, cost, qualification, and competitors. Economics affects pre-execution routing and post-finalization evidence only.

public_contracts.backward_compatibility: All PT8 configuration, bundle fields, and documents are additive. Unknown money/time/review remains null with explicit accounting; no numeric-zero migration is performed.

coverage: full and partial objective accounting plus conservative probability
determinism, sparse fallback, qualified/provisional/experimental eligibility, and no task-name leakage
privacy, provider, cost, permission, local-first, forced, strongest-only, reserves, and fallback ordering
credible retry and capability/no-progress escalation through existing controller regression coverage
future-only online update, infrastructure exclusion, forced metric exclusion, and false-acceptance quarantine
point-in-time replay, four scorecards, safe publication, refusal, active status, and rollback
legacy configuration/bundle migration without new side effects
read-only explain and economics CLI lifecycle
forced-policy metric exclusion and online-update contract

updated_integration_coverage: Web Agents tests show repository-specific conservative probability, exact-profile economics, partial component costs, and Unknown instead of zero.
Flight Recorder and Ops protocol suites validate all seven PT8 documents and reject fabricated zero unknown cost.
Controller tests prove immutable route-plan and validated online-update artifacts are referenced by the canonical manifest.

end_to_end_artifacts_and_screenshots.screenshot_reason: The in-app browser reported no available browser instance (inventory=[]). UI evidence is therefore 25 passing Web tests, typecheck, the 59-module production build, format check, and synchronized console-asset parity; no unrelated browser mechanism was substituted.

known_failures_and_skips: Gate C is INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE. The durable three-system qualification scorecards contain zero eligible founder observations, and the isolated PT8 Gate run has no configured scorecards.
The frozen PT8 replay input contains zero cases. All four strategy scorecards therefore have unknown cost/time/review/regret and cannot establish safe policy publication.
The two opt-in real-harness tests were skipped because VILLANI_REAL_HARNESS_TESTS was not set to 1; no paid credentials or execution were used.
Villani Ops skipped two existing native-Windows host-capability cases; Villani Code skipped one opt-in external smoke case.
No screenshot was captured because the in-app browser inventory was empty.
Preliminary full-suite, path, TEMP, formatting, lint, type, Web sandbox, controller serialization, and semantic-fixture failures were remediated or correctly rerun; final applicable suites pass.

security: Routing never authorizes acceptance and semantic verification stays blinded. A false-acceptance amendment quarantines qualification immediately, but raw local run artifacts and patches can still contain repository data and need operator review.
Worktree/process isolation is not a kernel sandbox. Privacy, permission, and unsupported environment constraints fail closed and must not be bypassed.
privacy: Local economics records include repository lineage, exact system/provider/model identity, timing, cost, and review metadata. Exports can reveal repository and provider information even when raw protocol logs are redacted.
Known provider cost is preserved authoritatively; unknown cost remains null. No credential values are stored in policy, route-plan, fixture, or report documents.
data_loss: Economics and qualification ledgers are append-only, policy publications are immutable, and rollback changes only the active pointer. Delivery still materializes only the selected recorded patch.
No user source, durable run evidence, vendor configuration, or session directory was deleted. Only the verified preview subprocess and disposable validation artifacts are cleaned up.
compatibility: Existing bundles and configuration remain readable. Legacy configurations do not silently enable online evidence writes; new initialized configuration does.
Exact system/provider/model/environment/policy/repository drift can intentionally make old economics ineligible or sparse, producing a safer fallback or no route rather than an optimistic estimate.
Cost comparison is currency-specific. Mixed or unknown currencies remain partial and cannot be ranked as complete accepted-change cost.

user_facing_behavior.before: Qualification could gate systems, but automatic routing did not persist a total accepted-change objective, component unknowns, economic fallback sequence, point-in-time policy comparison, or safe publication lifecycle.
user_facing_behavior.after: Villani chooses among qualified systems using conservative total cost per proven accepted change when inputs are complete, falls back to strongest evidence when sparse, never automatically selects experimental systems, and persists an explainable route plan. Agents shows repository-specific economics and Unknown values honestly. Advanced constraints remain hidden by default; policy explain is read-only; policy evaluation, publication, status, and rollback are deterministic and versioned.

cleanup.processes: The verified Vite preview process was terminated and port 4173 had no remaining listener.
cleanup.recovery: All 6 verified paths were removed permanently and a final check reported 6/6 absent. They were disposable configuration, Git fixture, replay, Gate, and preview-log artifacts; no durable PT8 evidence was in this list.
